authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: MINERAL COUNTY... Rose Creek Reservoir. Season: Open year around. Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Rose Creek Reservoir — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Stocking Updates: 195 Tiger Trout, 11.7 in, 07-09-2024; 201 Tiger Trout, 10.8 in, 06-21-2022.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Mineral County — Rose Creek Reservoir
regulations.humanSummary: Rose Creek Reservoir is open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: No species-specific sub-limit is stated for Rose Creek Reservoir specifically; limit applies to combined daily/possession total across all game fish species (this water is stocked primarily with tiger trout).

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Mineral County — Rose Creek Reservoir
regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 3 game fish; possession limit 6 game fish (all species combined) at Rose Creek Reservoir.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 3 game fish. Possession: 6 game fish.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Mineral County — Rose Creek Reservoir
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night at Rose Creek Reservoir.
regulations.verbatimText: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
